rxn_class_summary: Electro-oxidative three-component 1,2-amino oxygenation of alkenes with sulfonamides and trifluoroethanol (TFE) to give β-amino alcohol derivatives via N-centered radicals
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: DBU is electroactive and shuttles electrons from the sulfonamide anion (deprotonated by DBU) to the anode, generating an N-centered radical (NCR); the benzylic carbon radical formed after addition to alkene is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is trapped by TFE

key_intermediates.name: Benzylic carbon radical from NCR addition to styrene

electron_flow: DBU deprotonates the sulfonamide and is oxidized at the anode; electron transfer from sulfonamide anion to oxidized DBU generates an N-centered radical that adds to the alkene; the resulting benzylic radical is anodically oxidized to a cation and trapped by TFE to give the β-amino ether, with H2 evolved at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: Aliphatic sulfonamide 10 (non-benzylic competitive substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions with 3 equiv styrene, intermolecular 1,2-amino oxygenation product 10a formed in 15% while intramolecular pyrrolidine 10b (from 5-exo cyclization of NCR onto pendant alkene) formed in 41%, supporting an N-centered radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: DBU titration (0–2.0 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 4aa increased monotonically with DBU loading (0→76% at 2.0 equiv), consistent with DBU acting as base and electroactive mediator

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of DBU/1a/2aa
probe_reagents.observation: DBU shows catalytic current that increases with added sulfonamide 1a; 1a alone shows no oxidation peak 0–2.5 V; styrene 2aa is electroactive but EDG-styrenes give low yields, arguing against direct alkene oxidation as the initiation step

notes: Reviewers questioned the rigor of competitive experiments (benzylic C–H of substrate 10 could in principle be oxidized to a cation rather than going through NCR); authors addressed by using an aliphatic substrate where intramolecular 5-exo cyclization (10b, 41%) provides evidence for the NCR pathway. Reviewer #3 proposed an alternative: anodic oxidation of tosylamide anion directly to N-radical without DBU mediation. CV data indicate DBU mediation is operative rather than direct sulfonamide oxidation.
